source_pr.title: dxf/importinto: run table-empty check once per task
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #65954

Problem Summary:
After integrated the table mode feature #61200, we also added another round of table emptyness check, but for `IMPORT INTO` (classic mode), the target-table-empty check should be done at task start boundary, before starting import job execution, instead of inside later step-specific logic.

### What changed and how does it work?

- Moved the table-empty precheck to the beginning of `OnNextSubtasksBatch` in `pkg/dxf/importinto/scheduler.go`.
- Added guard conditions so it only runs when:
  - `kerneltype.IsClassic()`
  - `task.Step == proto.StepInit`
- Removed the old check from the `ImportStepImport/ImportStepEncodeAndSort` switch branch.
- This makes the check happen earlier in the first-step planning path and keeps later-step flow unchanged.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: In classic-mode `IMPORT INTO`, the target-table-emptiness check was performed again in later executor steps, so executor recreation/restart could re-run the check after partial import and fail intermittently. The PR moves the check to the task-start boundary (run once per task) and adds coverage for executor recreation, removing the restart-related race.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: diff shows determinism stabilization in tests/realtikvtest/importintotest/import_into_test.go
